initial_program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Determine grid dimensions
% ------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Row / column ranges for the output grid (same size as the input)
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Every cell of the output grid
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Rotated nonzero cells (before gravity)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
rot_nonzero(Rrot, Crot, Color) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Color),
    Color != 0,
    Rrot = Cin,
    Crot = N - 1 - Rin,
    Rrot >= 0, Rrot < M,
    Crot >= 0, Crot < N.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Gravity: compute the minimum row and column of the rotated shape
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shiftR(ShiftR) :- #min { R0 : rot_nonzero(R0, _, _), row(R0) }.
shiftC(ShiftC) :- #min { C0 : rot_nonzero(_, C0, _), col(C0) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Final nonzero output after gravity
% ------------------------------------------------------------
rot_to_output(R, C, Color) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Color),
    shiftR(ShiftR),
    shiftC(ShiftC),
    R = Cin - ShiftR,
    C = N - 1 - Rin - ShiftC,
    Color != 0,
    R >= 0, R < M,
    C >= 0, C < N,
    output(R, C, Color).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Does a nonzero output already exist at (R,C)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R, C) :- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Zero cells: cells without a nonzero output get colour0
output(R, C, 0) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    not has_nonzero(R, C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per output cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Determine grid dimensions
% ------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Row / column ranges for the output grid (same size as the input)
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Every cell of the output grid
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).


% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Gravity: compute the minimum row and column of the rotated shape
% ------------------------------------------------------------
valid_dims(N, M) :- n_rows(N), n_cols(M).

rot_nonzero(Rrot, Crot, Color) :- input(Rin, Cin, Color), Color != 0, Rrot = Cin, valid_dims(N, M), Crot = N - 1 - Rin, Rrot >= 0, Rrot < M, Crot >= 0, Crot < N.


% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Gravity: compute the minimum row and column of the rotated shape
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shiftR(ShiftR) :- shiftR_val(ShiftR).
shiftC(ShiftC) :- shiftC_val(ShiftC).
shiftR_val(ShiftR) :- #min { R0 : rot_nonzero(R0, _, _), row(R0) }.
shiftC_val(ShiftC) :- #min { C0 : rot_nonzero(_, C0, _), col(C0) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Final nonzero output after gravity
% ------------------------------------------------------------
rot_to_output(R, C, Color) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Color),
    shiftR(ShiftR),
    shiftC(ShiftC),
    R = Cin - ShiftR,
    C = N - 1 - Rin - ShiftC,
    Color != 0,
    R >= 0, R < M,
    C >= 0, C < N,
    output(R, C, Color).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Does a nonzero output already exist at (R,C)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R, C) :- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Zero cells: cells without a nonzero output get colour0
output(R, C, 0) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    not has_nonzero(R, C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per output cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Determine grid dimensions
% ------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Row / column ranges for the output grid (same size as the input)
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Every cell of the output grid
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).


% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Gravity: compute the minimum row and column of the rotated shape
% ------------------------------------------------------------
valid_dims(N, M) :- n_rows(N), n_cols(M).

rot_nonzero(Rrot, Crot, Color) :- input(Rin, Cin, Color), Color != 0, Rrot = Cin, valid_dims(N, M), Crot = N - 1 - Rin, Rrot >= 0, Rrot < M, Crot >= 0, Crot < N.


% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Gravity: compute the minimum row and column of the rotated shape
% ------------------------------------------------------------
shiftR(ShiftR) :- shiftR_val(ShiftR).
shiftC(ShiftC) :- shiftC_val(ShiftC).
shiftR_val(ShiftR) :- #min { R0 : rot_nonzero(R0, _, _), row(R0) }.
shiftC_val(ShiftC) :- #min { C0 : rot_nonzero(_, C0, _), col(C0) }.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Final nonzero output after gravity
% ------------------------------------------------------------
rot_to_output(R, C, Color) :-
    input(Rin, Cin, Color),
    shiftR(ShiftR),
    shiftC(ShiftC),
    R = Cin - ShiftR,
    C = N - 1 - Rin - ShiftC,
    Color != 0,
    R >= 0, R < M,
    C >= 0, C < N,
    output(R, C, Color).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Does a nonzero output already exist at (R,C)
% ------------------------------------------------------------
has_nonzero(R, C) :- output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Zero cells: cells without a nonzero output get colour0
output(R, C, 0) :-
    output_cell(R, C),
    not has_nonzero(R, C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per output cell
% ------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.
